ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for arrabbiata pasta with vegetables. FOODON_00002473 (pasta dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific arrabbiata or spicy tomato pasta code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for arrabbiata pasta with vegetables as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of arrabbiata pasta with vegetables is approximately 350 g, comprising a full plate of cooked penne in spicy tomato sauce with mixed vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Pasta, cooked, enriched, without added salt (USDA SR Legacy 20420) and Tomato sauce, marinara, ready-to-serve (USDA SR Legacy 11549), combined with vegetable component estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for arrabbiata pasta with vegetables as a composite dish. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for cooked enriched pasta (~55% by weight), tomato-based arrabbiata sauce (~30% by weight), and mixed sautéed vegetables (~15% by weight). Weighted blend applied per standard recipe proportions. Energy cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central composite pasta dish entries.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; pasta (penne, durum wheat, cooked) GI ~45
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for arrabbiata pasta with vegetables as a composite dish. GI of 45 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked penne/pasta (durum wheat, cooked), which is the dominant glycemic component (~55% by weight). Tomato sauce and vegetables contribute negligible additional glycemic load. Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) reports GI of cooked pasta in the range 40–50. GL per serving calculated as: (45 × 19.8 × 350) / (100 × 100) = 31.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100 g; ~735 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a standard restaurant serving of arrabbiata pasta with vegetables provides approximately 735 mg sodium, representing about 32% of the 2,300 mg daily limit; portion control and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~31 g per serving) from durum wheat pasta
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should note that arrabbiata pasta with vegetables has a low GI of approximately 45 but a moderate glycemic load of ~31 g per standard serving; portion reduction to half a plate and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables can help attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.4 g/100 g) and cholesterol-free profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Arrabbiata pasta with vegetables is a cholesterol-free, low-saturated-fat dish that is broadly compatible with a heart-healthy dietary pattern; however, sodium content should be monitored and olive oil quantity controlled to keep total fat intake within cardiovascular guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Arrabbiata Pasta with Vegetables is a spicy Italian-origin pasta dish adapted in the Singapore Indian restaurant context. The dish consists of penne or similar tubular pasta cooked al dente and tossed in a fiery arrabbiata sauce made from crushed tomatoes, garlic, red chilli, and olive oil, accompanied by sautéed mixed vegetables such as bell peppers, onions, zucchini, and broccoli. The name 'arrabbiata' derives from the Italian word for 'angry', reflecting the dish's characteristic heat from dried red chillies. The pasta is fully vegetarian and vegan, containing no meat, dairy, or eggs. Nutritionally, a 350 g serving provides approximately 413 kcal, 13 g protein, 10 g fat, and 69 g carbohydrate, with a low glycemic index of ~45 due to the durum wheat pasta base. The dish is a moderate source of dietary fibre (~6 g per serving) and provides useful amounts of vitamin C and vitamin A from the tomato sauce and vegetables. Sodium content is moderate at approximately 735 mg per serving, which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load per serving is approximately 31 g, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is halal-friendly and suitable for vegetarian and vegan diners. It is commonly served as a main course in Indian-Italian fusion restaurants in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in arrabbiata pasta?
Is arrabbiata pasta good for diabetics?
What is arrabbiata sauce made of?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for arrabbiata pasta with vegetables exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for cooked pasta, tomato sauce, and vegetables using standard recipe proportions. GI is based on the closest analogous food (cooked penne, durum wheat) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for cooked enriched pasta (FDC 20420), tomato-based sauce (FDC 11549), and mixed sautéed vegetables using a weighted blend (55% pasta, 30% sauce, 15% vegetables). 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero for plant-based pasta dish with olive oil; no animal fats present). 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero; dish is fully plant-based with no animal-derived ingredients). 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero; this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal products or fortification). 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for estimation, and plant-based pasta dishes do not contain meaningful vitamin D. 6. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on cooked penne (durum wheat) from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). GL per serving = (45 × 19.8 × 350) / 10000 = 31.2 g. 7. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input record is incorrect for this vegetarian pasta dish and has been disregarded. 8. The food is served at #Foodcoholic, 40 Circular Road, Singapore, via field survey at SGD 16.00. 9. Ontology codes use closest_parent matches as no exact arrabbiata pasta with vegetables codes exist in FoodOn or FoodEx2. 10. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singapore-served dishes.
